title: Your First Arpeggio: The p-i-m-a Pattern

cues.label: What an arpeggio is
cues.headline: A chord played one note at a time
cues.narration: An arpeggio is a chord played in sequence, its notes unfolded one at a time instead of together. The word comes from the Italian arpeggiare, to play as a harp.

cues.label: The p-i-m-a cycle on Am
cues.headline: Thumb root, then i-m-a on 3-2-1
cues.narration: Hold A minor. The thumb, p, plucks string five, the A root; then i plucks string three, m plucks string two, and a plucks string one. Four notes, one cycle, then repeat.

cues.label: Keep the thumb independent
cues.headline: Bass reaches while fingers stay home
cues.narration: While the thumb reaches down for the bass string, i, m and a stay lightly on their treble strings. The motions do not interfere, and that independence is what makes the pattern musical instead of mechanical.
